_comment: Per-scenario baseline observations captured on main HEAD before any uplift
code change lands. User edits this file after running each fixture through
the 04-Design agent in Copilot Chat. Run `node tools/scripts/capture-drawio-baseline.mjs`
after every entry to regenerate regen-baseline.json.

g1-three-tier-web.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective edits). Friction events captured: (1) MCP infra setup [1 failed search-shapes + 6 debug terminal calls before retry succeeded]; (2) redundant finish-diagram preview before compressed export; (3) post-export palette correction via sed (#EFF6FF/#0078D4 -> #E7F5FF/#6C8EBF) instead of MCP edit-cells. Visual quality observed below 3/4 acceptance bar (rubric mean ~2.86).
quality_issues: Edge-label collision: 'SqlHTTPS' visible — two parallel edges (HTTPS to Storage and SQL/TDS to Azure SQL DB) had labels overlap into a fused string. Pain point #5.
Missing Managed Identity edges per prompt: prompt requires Web App -> Key Vault and Web App -> SQL via Managed Identity; only one Web App -> SQL edge rendered. Pain point #5.

Application Insights and Log Analytics Workspace placed below the resource group with no zone label and no telemetry edges from Web App. No 'Observability' or cross-cutting zone visible. Pain point #4.
No trust-boundary marker at the End User -> Web App public ingress. Pain point #4.
Web App -> Storage and Web App -> SQL edges visually overlap before fan-out, contributing to label collision. Pain point #2.

g2-hub-spoke-landing-zone.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective edits). Friction events captured: (1) failed read_file on references/semantic-zones.md [T-019 deferred dependency; ref does not yet exist] + 5 recovery terminal calls; (2) two search-shapes calls instead of one batched call (skill convention violated); (3) 8 terminal calls extracting placeholder XML between add-cells and finish-diagram (G1 needed 4 - extra XML mining); (4) post-export palette correction via sed (#E6F2FA -> #e6f5e6) - same pattern as G1. Improvement vs G1: single finish-diagram call (no preview+final). Source: Copilot Chat session e88ecb6b-2df2-4b73-a033-f05c8bbe3368.
quality_issues: Paired-shape label collisions: 'Web App 1Web App 2', 'Key Vault (PE)Storage (PE)', 'SQL MI (PL Cosmos DB (PL)' — sibling cells in subnet groups positioned too close (same bug family as G1's SqlHTTPS edge collision). Pain points #2, #5.
Trust Boundary marker positioned outside the On-Premises Network container instead of visually wrapping the perimeter — boundary intent reads as a label rather than a zone. Pain point #4.

Failed dependency: agent attempted to read `references/semantic-zones.md` per prompt link, but the file does not yet exist (T-019 deferred). Surfaces as 1 friction event + 5 recovery calls. Pain point #4 (missing skill reference).

Skill-convention violation: agent issued two separate `search-shapes` calls (initial 18 queries + follow-up 4 queries) instead of one batched call as required by SKILL.md. Pain point #1 (workflow drift).
Palette enforcement is too late: validator catches drift only post-export; agent corrects via out-of-band `sed -i` instead of MCP `edit-cells`. Same workflow as G1. Pain point #3.

g3-event-driven-microservices.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch with 24 cells, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective edits). Friction events: (1) THREE search-shapes calls (9+4+3 queries) instead of one batch — skill-convention drift worsening vs G2 (which had 2); (2) post-export palette correction via sed (#e6f2fa -> #dae8fc) — same pattern as G1/G2; (3) create_file at [25] writing an artifact outside the standard save-drawio.py flow. Improvements: only 2 XML-mining calls between add-cells and finish-diagram (G1=3, G2=8). Sequence type correctly identified — legend correctly omitted per OQ-2 carve-out. Source: Copilot Chat session 16bcee18-21df-4f58-8228-8a26bb005a65.
quality_issues: REGRESSION (Layout 1/4): 50% of canvas empty — App Insights + Log Analytics floating bottom-left with no observability zone, no telemetry edges from any Container App. Same floating cross-cutting pattern as G1. Pain point #2, #4.

REGRESSION (Labelling 1/4): Multiple fused edge labels — 'HTTPS / OAuth2HTTPS / OAuth2' (Client->APIM and APIM->orders-api parallel labels merged), 'AMQPAMQPHTTPS :443' (3-way merge), 'HTTPS :443LS :6380' (TLS truncated/merged with port). This is the SqlHTTPS bug from G1 amplified across multiple parallel edges. Pain point #5, #2.

WRONG EDGE TARGET: 'change feed' edge drawn from Cosmos DB to Redis Cache (should target Event Grid per prompt). Indicates agent loses semantic intent during edge layout. Pain point #5 (semantic correctness).
Skill-convention drift WORSENING: 3 separate search-shapes calls (G1=2, G2=2, G3=3) — agent is splitting batches more, not less. Pain point #1 (workflow drift).
Trust boundary missing at APIM public ingress per prompt. Pain point #4.

Bright side: Type-fit 4/4 — agent correctly identified sequence/runtime type and applied logical zones (Ingress/Processing/Persistence) instead of forcing VNet/network template. Validates the eventual T-015/T-016 type-dispatch design.

g4-ml-training-pipeline.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch with 16 cells; the edit-cells at [12] is a pre-batch group-label correction, not a retry under the rule). Friction events: (1) FOUR search-shapes calls (10+8+6+7=31 queries) — drift continues monotonically G1=2 G2=2 G3=3 G4=4; (2) group-label correction via edit-cells after create-groups (forgot to set titles); (3) multiple python XML-extraction round-trips between add-cells and add-cells-to-group; (4) post-export palette correction via Python re.sub (escalation from sed) — same chronic pattern. Improvements: first scenario to use create-groups + add-cells-to-group containment workflow (proper). Source: Copilot Chat session cbc7eb7b-872b-44af-ac07-82249a9051e4.
quality_issues: REGRESSION (Labelling 1/4): 'AMIAML SDKace' triple-label fusion on the AML Workspace icon — three separate labels overlapped into a single illegible string. This is the worst label collision observed across G1-G4 and confirms the pattern is amplifying with workflow complexity (groups + edges + multiple resources at one point). Pain point #5.

REGRESSION (Layout 1/4): 60% of canvas empty. Cross-cutting items (Key Vault, Application Insights, Log Analytics) placed in a useless mid-zone with no observability container, no trust boundary, no edges to compute. Same pattern as G1/G3, more pronounced. Pain points #2, #4.

Variant requirement partly met: GPU Cluster labelled '(A100)', ADLS Gen2 labelled '(HNS)', ACR labelled 'Premium' — variant info is in TEXT labels but icons themselves are generic family icons. Rubric anchor 3/4: 'correct families, but stale icon-set version' applies. Pain point #1.
Missing zones: prompt expected 'ML Workspace' and 'Data Zone' (delivered) but security/observability zone for KV+AI+LA absent. Pain point #4.

Skill-convention drift WORSENING: search-shapes call counts G1=2, G2=2, G3=3, G4=4 — strictly monotonic. Each scenario splits batches more than the last. SKILL.md 'single batch' rule is being eroded as scenario complexity increases. Pain point #1 (workflow drift).

Bright spots: (a) first scenario to use create-groups + add-cells-to-group containment workflow correctly, (b) best legend yet — accurately enumerates variant rationale (GPU=A100, ADLS=Gen2, ACR=Premium) and edge meanings (AML SDK / ABFS / Pull). Validates T-021 + T-028 legend design.

g5-enterprise-landing-zone.observations: FIRST SCENARIO TO TRIGGER THE STRICT RETRY RULE. Tool sequence: search-shapes [7] -> search-shapes [8] -> create-groups [10] -> add-cells [12] -> add-cells-to-group [15] -> add-cells-to-group [20] (recovery) -> finish-diagram [27] -> CLEAR-DIAGRAM [29] -> create-groups [30] -> add-cells [31] -> add-cells-to-group [35] -> finish-diagram [38] -> finish-diagram [46] (final). retries=1 (clear-diagram + full rebuild at [29]-[31]). Friction events: (1) 2 search-shapes calls; (2) duplicate add-cells-to-group at [15] and [20]; (3) two intermediate finish-diagram round-trips ([27], [38]) before final at [46]; (4) format-debugging cycles ([41]-[45]) including comparing to G1 diagram format; (5) post-validation cleanup loop. Cost = 1 retry + 5 friction = 6 (highest so far). Source: Copilot Chat session e6e5cc83-34bd-419e-bceb-002159ff6aa4.
quality_issues: STRICT RETRY TRIGGERED: clear-diagram + full rebuild at [29]-[31]. Agent built diagram, found it unsatisfactory, rebuilt from scratch — exactly the strict-rule pattern. First scenario to do so. Validates that the strict rule does fire at higher complexity even with current agent behaviour.

MG hierarchy STRUCTURALLY INCORRECT: prompt specifies Tenant Root MG -> {Platform MG -> {Connectivity MG, Identity MG}, Landing Zones MG, Sandbox MG}. Diagram shows Connectivity MG and Identity MG as peers of Platform/Landing Zones/Sandbox. Two-level nesting collapsed to one. Pain point #4 (semantics).

Subscription column header collisions: 'Connectivity MG hub VNet 10.0.0.0/16', 'Identity MG... Subscription', 'M VNet Peering ID' fragment near Identity. Same paired-label collision family as G2-G4 but applied to subscription headers. Pain points #2, #5.

AKS / ACR duplication: prompt has one AKS cluster + ACR Premium. Diagram shows AKS (Private, CNI) + AKS (Shared) + Container Registry + ACR (Shared) — misinterpreted shared services in App Shared subscription. Pain point #5 (semantic correctness).

Cross-subscription edge violation: bidirectional VNet Peering arrow drawn as a long line from Connectivity column to App Workload column, crossing through Identity Subscription and App Shared Subscription containers. Violates 'edges should not cross unrelated containers'. Pain point #2.
Trust Boundary in legend only — symbol described but no actual boundary rendered. Pain point #4.

REGRESSION (Scalability 1/4): first scenario at the >20-resource decomposition threshold. T-023 reference does not yet exist; agent attempted self-decomposition (clear+rebuild) but ended up with a single-page result that fails layout. Bottom 50% of canvas still empty. Validates T-023 priority.

Bright spots: (a) first capture to TRIGGER THE STRICT RETRY RULE (validates Option C measurement design — strict still fires at the right complexity); (b) good legend covering MG / subscription scope / peering / private link / trust boundary symbol; (c) no out-of-band sed/regex palette correction this time (palette was correct from add-cells).

g6-hyperscale-platform.observations: FIRST SCENARIO TO DELIVER MULTI-PAGE DECOMPOSITION. Three sequential page builds (Overview / Sweden Central / Germany West Central), each its own create-groups -> add-cells -> add-cells-to-group -> finish-diagram -> save. Per-page cells: 30 / 24 / 24, all within T-023 tier `>50` ceiling (<=30). Strict retries=0 (no clear-diagram, no full rebuild — sequential page builds are NOT retries under the rule because each page is a distinct batch on a clean slate). Friction events: (1) 2 search-shapes calls; (2) page-1 XML-extraction round-trips; (3) add-cells-to-group called twice on page 1 (recovery from missing assignments); (4) page-2 XML-extraction round-trips; (5) page-3 XML-extraction round-trips; (6) custom Python ElementTree multi-page merger at [47] — agent had to write its own merger because MCP server doesn't support multi-page diagrams natively. Cost = 0 + 6 = 6 (tied with G5 but G5 was failing; G6 is succeeding at a harder task). Source: Copilot Chat session 808477bd-80cb-42ac-b881-8e0e3c3a249f.
quality_issues: BREAKTHROUGH: first scenario to score 4/4 on Scalability AND 4/4 on Type-fit. Multi-page decomposition delivered correctly: 3 pages (Overview / Sweden Central / Germany West Central) with per-page cell counts 30/24/24, all within the T-023 tier `>50` ceiling of 30. Validates T-008 (type-fit signature) and T-023 (decomposition rules) priority — the agent CAN do this today, just at high friction cost.
BREAKTHROUGH: rubric mean 3.14/4 — first capture above the 3/4 acceptance bar. Confirms hypothesis that decomposed diagrams have room for layout/labelling to breathe.

HTML-entity bug in legend text: '
' literal sequences appear in the legend cell value instead of being rendered as newlines. Legend reads as one long line: 'Legend
Blue = Sweden Central (Primary)
Green = Germany West Central...'. Same family as G3 fused labels (text-rendering pipeline issue). Pain point #5.

Edge-label collisions on global edges: 'Multi-master repli Multi-master replication' (label appears twice fused) and 'Front Door routing' stacked on Cosmos+EventHubs. Less severe than G2-G5 due to fewer parallel edges per endpoint. Pain point #5.

MCP server gap exposed: agent had to write a custom Python ElementTree merger to combine the 3 single-page outputs into one multi-page .drawio. Validates a NEW workstream — the MCP server's finish-diagram tool is single-page-only. Should be tracked as proposed multi-page-finish capability.

Custom-merge approach is fragile: relies on element order, namespace handling, and string concatenation. A native multi-page mode in finish-diagram would eliminate friction event #6 (~17% of the total).
Trust boundary missing at Front Door public ingress (same as G3). Pain point #4.
Cross-cutting drift continues: Sentinel + Defender for Cloud floating bottom of overview page with no observability zone. Pain point #4.

g7-multi-region-active-active.observations: Strict retries=0 (1 add-cells batch with 24 cells, 0 clear-diagram, 0 corrective edits). Friction events: (1) XML-extraction round-trips between add-cells and add-cells-to-group; (2) XML-extraction round-trips between add-cells-to-group and finish-diagram; (3) post-validation regex correction at [23] (palette warning). Cost = 0 + 3 = 3, tied with G1 and G3 for lowest. Notable: FIRST SCENARIO SINCE G2 to obey the single-batched search-shapes rule (G3=3 calls, G4=4 calls, G5=2 calls, G6=2 calls, G7=1 call). Skill-batching drift is NOT monotonic — correlates with prompt shape-variety, not session age. G7 has only 8 distinct shapes, fewest of any G3-G7 scenario. Source: Copilot Chat session 63a11d98-964b-4e52-ab75-f07dde54a8a6.
quality_issues: BEST CAPTURE: rubric mean 3.43/4 — second above-bar scenario after G6 (3.14/4). G7 nails type-fit (4/4) and scalability (4/4) without needing decomposition. Validates that medium-complexity prompts with explicit zone/edge cues produce strong output today.

Region symmetry near-perfect: Sweden Central and Germany West Central columns mirror with paired Web App, Cosmos, Storage, Key Vault, App Insights at exact alignment. No paired-label collision because each region has 1 of each (no internal pairs to collide).

Replication edges are first-class: 'Multi-region writes' (Cosmos↔Cosmos dashed bidirectional), 'Geo-replication' (Storage↔Storage dashed) — explicit, labelled, no fusion. First scenario where complex edges DON'T collide.

Mild DNS+HTTPS label collision at top: 'HTTPS (weighted) DNS' fragment where DNS-Zone label and Front-Door routing edge share an endpoint and labels stack. Less severe than G2-G6 because only 2 edges share the endpoint.

Telemetry edge direction ambiguous: arrow drawn between the two App Insights instances with label '↑Telemetry'. Prompt says shared Log Analytics in Sweden Central; agent rendered cross-region App Insights link instead of App→AppInsights→LA. Pain point #5 (semantic correctness, minor).
Trust boundary still missing at Front Door public ingress (same as G3, G5, G6). Pain point #4.

Inline legend: '→ HTTPS weighted routing | ↔ Multi-region writes (Cosmos DB) | ↔ Geo-replication (Storage GZRS) | → Telemetry' — concise, encodes edge meanings, sits below the diagram. Different format from G4/G6 (vertical list) but equally readable.
Bright signal: NO empty canvas — first scenario since G2 with good density and balanced layout. Validates that single-region or 2-region medium prompts (15-20 resources) are the agent's sweet spot.
